symptom_vector: Client URL misconfigured, code error, runtime panic leading to service failures

expert_knowledge.root_cause_desc: Component [frontend] experienced [pod_processes] fault, accompanied by [timeout] anomaly.
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Check Metadata - Confirm faulty component is [frontend] (From Ground Truth instance field)
Step 2: Check Observation - Found keyword [http.resp.status, 302] in Log (From GT key_observations)
Step 3: Check Observation - Found keyword [request, response, timeout] in Metric (From GT key_observations)
Step 4: Check Observation - Found keyword [pod_cpu_usage] in Metric (From GT key_observations)
Step 5: Check Observation - Found keyword [pod_processes] in Metric (From GT key_observations)
Step 6: Derive Root Cause - Based on above features, determined cause is [pod_processes timeout] (From Ground Truth key_metrics)

critical_checks.expected_pattern: HTTP 302 status codes appearing in logs indicating redirects or misconfigurations
critical_checks.instruction: Search for keyword 'http.resp.status' with value '302' in log entries during fault timeframe

critical_checks.expected_pattern: Abnormal request/response patterns and timeout metrics in service telemetry
critical_checks.instruction: Check metric data for anomalies in request count, response count, and timeout occurrences

critical_checks.expected_pattern: Abnormal CPU usage patterns for frontend pods
critical_checks.instruction: Monitor pod_cpu_usage metrics for frontend service instances during fault period

critical_checks.expected_pattern: Abnormal process count or behavior in frontend pods
critical_checks.instruction: Check pod_processes metrics for frontend service to detect process-related anomalies
